briefdescription.para: Not enough space in output buffer.
Predefined values for some standard encodings.
Create converter for input (conversion to UTF-8)
Create converter for output (conversion from UTF-8)

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* xmlCharEncodingInputFunc) (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.argsstring: )(unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.name: xmlCharEncodingInputFunc

detaileddescription.para: On success, the value of inlen after return is the number of bytes consumed and outlen is the number of bytes produced.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the UTF-8 result
a pointer to an array of chars in the original encoding

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* xmlCharEncodingOutputFunc) (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.argsstring: )(unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.name: xmlCharEncodingOutputFunc

detaileddescription.para: On success, the value of inlen after return is the number of bytes consumed and outlen is the number of bytes produced.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of UTF-8 chars

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlCharEncError(* xmlCharEncConvFunc) (void *vctxt, unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen, int flush)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *vctxt, unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen, int flush)
memberdef.name: xmlCharEncConvFunc

detaileddescription.para: The value of inlen after return is the number of bytes consumed and outlen is the number of bytes produced.
If the converter can consume partial multi-byte sequences, the flush flag can be used to detect truncated sequences at EOF. Otherwise, the flag can be ignored.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of input bytes

simplesect.para: an xmlCharEncError code.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlParserErrors(* xmlCharEncConvImpl) (void *vctxt, const char *name, xmlCharEncFlags flags, xmlCharEncodingHandler **out)
memberdef.name: xmlCharEncConvImpl

briefdescription.para: If this function returns XML_ERR_OK, it must fill the out pointer with an encoding handler.

detaileddescription.para: The handler can be obtained from xmlCharEncNewCustomHandler.
flags can contain XML_ENC_INPUT, XML_ENC_OUTPUT or both.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the memory allocated for the char encoding support, it unregisters all the encoding handlers and the aliases.

xrefdescription.para: This function will be made private. Call xmlCleanupParser to free global state but see the warnings there. xmlCleanupParser should be only called once at program exit. In most cases, you don't have call cleanup functions at all.
This function modifies global state and is not thread-safe. See xmlCtxtSetCharEncConvImpl for an alternative.

briefdescription.para: Find or create a handler matching the encoding.

detaileddescription.para: The following converters are looked up in order:
The handler must be closed with xmlCharEncCloseFunc.
If the encoding is UTF-8, a NULL handler and no error code will be returned.

simplesect.para: XML_ERR_OK, XML_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ENCODING or another xmlParserErrors error code.

briefdescription.para: Find or create a handler matching the encoding.

detaileddescription.para: The following converters are looked up in order:
The handler must be closed with xmlCharEncCloseFunc.
If the encoding is UTF-8, a NULL handler and no error code will be returned.

parameterdescription.para: a string describing the char encoding.

simplesect.para: XML_ERR_OK, XML_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ENCODING or another xmlParserErrors error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *name, xmlCharEncFlags flags, xmlCharEncConvImpl impl, void *implCtxt, xmlCharEncodingHandler **out)
memberdef.name: xmlCreateCharEncodingHandler

briefdescription.para: Find or create a handler matching the encoding.

detaileddescription.para: The following converters are looked up in order:
The handler must be closed with xmlCharEncCloseFunc.
If the encoding is UTF-8, a NULL handler and no error code will be returned.
flags can contain XML_ENC_INPUT, XML_ENC_OUTPUT or both.

parameterdescription.para: a string describing the char encoding.

simplesect.para: XML_ERR_OK, XML_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ENCODING or another xmlParserErrors error code.

xrefdescription.para: Use xmlLookupCharEncodingHandler which has better error reporting.

simplesect.para: the handler or NULL if no handler was found or an error occurred.

briefdescription.para: If the encoding is UTF-8, this will return a no-op handler that shouldn't be used.

xrefdescription.para: Use xmlOpenCharEncodingHandler which has better error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: a string describing the char encoding.

simplesect.para: the handler or NULL if no handler was found or an error occurred.

xrefdescription.para: This function modifies global state and is not thread-safe. See xmlCtxtSetCharEncConvImpl for an alternative.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding name, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)
the xmlCharEncodingInputFunc to read that encoding
the xmlCharEncodingOutputFunc to write that encoding

simplesect.para: the xmlCharEncodingHandler created (or NULL in case of error).

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *name, xmlCharEncConvFunc input, xmlCharEncConvFunc output, xmlCharEncConvCtxtDtor ctxtDtor, void *inputCtxt, void *outputCtxt, xmlCharEncodingHandler **out)
memberdef.name: xmlCharEncNewCustomHandler

parameterdescription.para: input callback which converts to UTF-8
output callback which converts from UTF-8

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Registers an alias alias for an encoding named name.

detaileddescription.para: Existing aliases will be overwritten.

xrefdescription.para: This function modifies global state and is not thread-safe. See xmlCtxtSetCharEncConvImpl for an alternative.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding name as parsed, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)
the alias name as parsed, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error.

xrefdescription.para: This function modifies global state and is not thread-safe. See xmlCtxtSetCharEncConvImpl for an alternative.

parameterdescription.para: the alias name as parsed, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Lookup an encoding name for the given alias.

parameterdescription.para: the alias name as parsed, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)

simplesect.para: NULL if not found, otherwise the original name.

xrefdescription.para: This function modifies global state and is not thread-safe. See xmlCtxtSetCharEncConvImpl for an alternative.

briefdescription.para: Compare the string to the encoding schemes already known.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the comparison is case insensitive accordingly to the section [XML] 4.3.3 Character Encoding in Entities.

parameterdescription.para: the encoding name as parsed, in UTF-8 format (ASCII actually)

simplesect.para: one of the xmlCharEncoding values or XML_CHAR_ENCODING_NONE if not recognized.

detaileddescription.para: C.f. http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-xml#charencoding Section 4.3.3 Character Encoding in Entities

simplesect.para: the canonical name for the given encoding.

briefdescription.para: Guess the encoding of the entity using the first bytes of the entity content according to the non-normative appendix F of the XML-1.0 recommendation.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the first bytes of the XML entity, must be at least 2 bytes long (at least 4 if encoding is UTF4 variant).
pointer to the length of the buffer

simplesect.para: a xmlCharEncoding value.

briefdescription.para: Generic front-end for output encoding conversion.

detaileddescription.para: A first call with in set to NULL has to be made to write a BOM.
When using GNU libiconv, unsupported characters in the output encoding will be automatically replaced with a numeric character reference.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.

briefdescription.para: Generic front-end for input encoding conversion.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.
the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.

detaileddescription.para: Must be called after a handler is no longer in use.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.name: xmlUTF8ToIsolat1

briefdescription.para: Take a block of UTF-8 chars in and try to convert it to an ISO Latin 1 block of chars out.

detaileddescription.para: The value of inlen after return is the number of bytes consumed. The value of outlen after return is the number of bytes produced.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of UTF-8 chars

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.name: xmlIsolat1ToUTF8

briefdescription.para: Take a block of ISO Latin 1 chars in and try to convert it to an UTF-8 block of chars out.

detaileddescription.para: The value of inlen after return is the number of bytes consumed. The value of outlen after return is the number of bytes produced.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of ISO Latin 1 chars

simplesect.para: the number of bytes written or an xmlCharEncError code.
See Copyright for the status of this software.
Daniel Veillard
